statement: Any two groups of order `3` are isomorphic.
lean_statement:
theorem iso_of_card_three
    {G H : Type*} [Group G] [Fintype G] [Group H] [Fintype H]
    (hG : Fintype.card G = 3) (hH : Fintype.card H = 3) :
    Nonempty (G ≃* H)
proof:
Proof: since `3` is prime, any group of order `3` is cyclic: pick `g` not equal to `1`, then the
subgroup
`⟨g⟩` has order dividing `3` and not equal to `1`, hence it has order `3` and equals the whole
group. Thus both `G` and `H` are cyclic of order `3`. Choose generators `g` of `G` and `h` of `H`;
then the map sending `g^n` to `h^n` is a well-defined group isomorphism `G ≃* H`.
